sales.description:
Ancients
SASANIAN KINGDOM. Shahpur (Sabuhr) I the Great (AD 240-272). AV dinar (21mm, 7.41 gm, 3h). NGC MS 5/5 - 4/5. Mint I ("Ctesiphon"), Phase 2, ca. AD 260-272. Draped bust of Shahpur I right, wearing mural tiara with korymbos and long ear flap / Fire altar flanked by two attendants standing facing, turned outwards, wearing mural crown, grounded scepter in outer hand; three pellets below elbow of right attendant. Sunrise 739 var. (pellets). SNS type IIc/1b. Göbl type I/1.  www.HA.com/Texas...

SASANIAN KINGDOM. Shahpur (Sabuhr) II (AD 309-379). AV dinar (20mm, 7.22 gm, 2h). NGC AU 3/5 - 4/5.  Uncertain mint, ca. AD 320. Bust of Shahpur II right, seen from front, wearing mural crown
sales.description:
Ancients
SASANIAN KINGDOM. Shahpur (Sabuhr) II (AD 309-379). AV dinar (20mm, 7.22 gm, 2h). NGC AU 3/5 - 4/5.  Uncertain mint, ca. AD 320. Bust of Shahpur II right, seen from front, wearing mural crown with korymbos / Fire altar tied with ribbons; flanked by incense burners. Sunrise 863. Göbl Ia/7.

Shahpur II (alternatively Sabuhr) reigned from 309 AD to 379 AD, the longest tenure in Iranian history, initiating a Golden Age of prosperity and military success for his empire. After his campaigns...
